title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

seo.title: Authentic Focaccia Genovese Recipe | Rosemary & Olive Oil
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Focaccia Genovese with rosemary & olive oil! Our easy recipe guides you to a perfect, airy loaf. Ideal for sharing or dipping.
longTailKeywords: how to make Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil
Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil step by step
seo.h1: Authentic Focaccia Genovese with Rosemary & Olive Oil
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Focaccia Genovese
Pro Tips for the Best Focaccia Genovese Results
Variations and Substitutions for Focaccia Genovese
seo.imageAlt: Close-up of golden Focaccia Genovese with fresh rosemary and olive oil drizzle, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the taste of Italy with this homemade Focaccia Genovese, featuring aromatic rosemary and rich olive oil.

description: Indulge in the rustic charm of Focaccia Genovese, a classic Italian flatbread from Genoa. This recipe highlights the simple yet profound flavors of fresh rosemary and rich olive oil, creating an incredibly soft, airy interior with a perfectly crisp crust. It's an ideal accompaniment to any meal or a delightful snack on its own.

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water and yeast. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active. Add 2 tablespoons of olive oil to the mixture.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Add the flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. The dough will be quite soft.

instructions.title: First Rise
instructions.text: Lightly grease a clean bowl with olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel and let it rise in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Prepare for Baking
instructions.text: Generously oil a 9x13 inch (23x33 cm) baking pan with extra virgin olive oil. Gently transfer the risen dough to the prepared pan. Using your fingertips, gently stretch the dough to fill the pan, being careful not to deflate it too much. Let it rest for 15 minutes.

instructions.title: Second Rise and Dimpling
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 425F (220C). After the rest, use your oiled fingertips to create deep dimples all over the surface of the focaccia dough. Drizzle generously with more olive oil, scatter the fresh rosemary, and sprinkle with flaky sea salt. Let it rise for another 20-30 minutes while the oven preheats.

instructions.title: Bake the Focaccia
instructions.text: Bake in the preheated oven for 20-30 minutes, or until the focaccia is golden brown and cooked through. If the top browns too quickly, you can loosely tent it with foil. Once baked, remove from the oven and transfer to a wire rack to cool slightly before slicing and serving.

tips: For an extra crispy crust, drizzle a little more olive oil over the focaccia just before baking. The oil helps to fry the bottom slightly.
If you dont have fresh rosemary, dried rosemary can be used, but reduce the quantity by half as its more potent. You can also experiment with other herbs like oregano or thyme.
Focaccia is best enjoyed warm on the day it is made. Store any leftovers at room temperature in an airtight container for up to 2 days, or freeze for longer storage.
article: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is a beloved Italian Baking dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
